title: TODOs.json task
description: Schema for a single task object in TODOs.json. The file root is an array of these objects.

id.description: Sequential identifier in the form todo-N. Assigned by append-tasks.sh based on the highest existing id.

source.description: Stable origin identity for the task, used to de-duplicate on re-run. Format: <origin>:<stable-key>, where origin is the source file basename (or 'prose') and stable-key is a slug of the source item (PRD row descriptor, open-question text, or prose work item). Two tasks with the same source are the same task; append-tasks.sh drops a re-derived task whose source already exists.

status.description: Lifecycle state. New tasks always start as todo.

agents.description: Free-text hint about which agent or skill should handle the task. Null when no specific agent applies.

description.description: Specific, action-oriented task description. Starts with a verb.

examples.description: Implement per-user rate limiting middleware with per-endpoint configuration
Write migration documentation for v2 API breaking changes
